metaSummary: Pokémon Champions — Regulation Set M-B en vigueur depuis le 16 juin 2026 (jusqu'au 1er septembre). Les Pokémon de la M-A restent jouables, rejoints par cinq nouvelles Méga-Évolutions : Mega Sceptile, Mega Blaziken, Mega Swampert, Mega Mawile et Mega Staraptor. Le jeu est disponible sur iOS et Android, avec la saison Ranked M-3 (17 juin – 8 juillet) et un nouveau Battle Pass. Les premiers repères de la méta M-B viennent du VR June Challenge #2 (premier gros tournoi en Reg. M-B, 174 joueurs), remporté par Louis Markl : Mega Charizard Y reste la Méga la plus jouée (29,3 %) devant Mega Floette (25,3 %), tandis que les nouveautés Mega Swampert (20,1 %) et Mega Staraptor (19,0 %) s'installent déjà dans le top. En usage général, Garchomp, Basculegion et Whimsicott dominent. Sur le circuit officiel, le NAIC 2026 (Nouvelle-Orléans, dernier événement en Reg. M-A) a été remporté par Francesco Pio Pero et la Taiwan Master Ball League (Taichung) par Chen Yu-Wei. Le Monthly Challenge Series de juin (26–29 juin, en ligne, simple, Reg. M-B) s'est conclu, remporté par Kaminchu. Victory Road a annoncé The Champions Arena II (4–5 juillet, en ligne, Reg. M-B), avant les événements live de l'été qualificatifs pour les Mondiaux 2026 à San Francisco (28–30 août).
metaSummary_en: Pokémon Champions — Regulation Set M-B is in effect from 16 June 2026 to 1 September. All Regulation M-A Pokémon remain eligible, joined by five new Mega Evolutions: Mega Sceptile, Mega Blaziken, Mega Swampert, Mega Mawile and Mega Staraptor. The game is available on iOS and Android, with Ranked Battles Season M-3 (17 June – 8 July) and a new Battle Pass underway. The first read on the M-B meta comes from the VR June Challenge #2 (the first major in Reg. M-B, 174 players), won by Louis Markl: Mega Charizard Y remains the most-used Mega (29.3%) ahead of Mega Floette (25.3%), while newcomers Mega Swampert (20.1%) and Mega Staraptor (19.0%) are already in the top tier. In general usage, Garchomp, Basculegion and Whimsicott lead the field. On the official circuit, the 2026 NAIC (New Orleans, the last event under Reg. M-A) was won by Francesco Pio Pero and the Taiwan Master Ball League (Taichung) by Chen Yu-Wei. June's Monthly Challenge Series (26–29 June, online, single battles, Reg. M-B) has wrapped up, won by Kaminchu. Victory Road has announced The Champions Arena II (4–5 July, online, Reg. M-B), ahead of the summer live tournaments that qualify players for the 2026 World Championships in San Francisco (28–30 August).

newsFeed: Regulation Set M-B is live (16 June – 1 Sept 2026): new Megas including Sceptile, Blaziken, Swampert, Mawile and Staraptor.
First Reg. M-B usage in: at the VR June Challenge #2, Mega Charizard Y (29.3%) leads, with new Megas Swampert (20.1%) and Staraptor (19.0%) already in the top tier.
Garchomp, Basculegion and Whimsicott top general usage in early Reg. M-B play.
Louis Markl wins the VR June Challenge #2, the first big Regulation M-B event.
Monthly Challenge Series June 2026 (online, single battles, Reg. M-B) has concluded, won by Kaminchu.
The Champions Arena II announced for 4–5 July 2026 (online, Reg. M-B); sign-ups are open.
Ranked Battles Season M-3 (17 June – 8 July) and a new Battle Pass are underway.
Pokémon Champions is now available on iOS and Android.
2026 World Championships set for San Francisco, 28–30 August.
